title: Alyeska Resort Spring Carnival
description: Three-day end-of-ski-season festival celebrating 49 years with the Alaska Airlines Slush Cup, costume contest, Mini-Midway carnival games, live music from Brother Buffalo and Andy Frasco & The U.N., and a beer garden. Note: dates span late April but are a key regional draw.

title: Anchorage Museum: Michaela Goade Exhibition Opening
description: New exhibition "Michaela Goade: Song of the Seasons" opens at the Anchorage Museum, alongside "Everyday Anchorage: Duke Russell & Ward Wells" and "AK Grown: Childhood in Alaska."

title: First Friday Art Walk — May
description: Monthly gallery walk with openings at Crimson (Bore Tide photography by Kerry Tasker), Turnagain Ceramics (community pottery throw), Bivy (A Constructed World exhibition), Visit Anchorage (Hope Studios), and many more venues.

title: Bivy Gallery: 'My Voice Keeps Changing on Me' Exhibition
description: Interactive exhibition and performance by A Constructed World, featuring installation, painting, and video. Opens during First Friday from 5–9pm.

title: Anchorage Museum Free First Friday — May
description: Free admission evening at the Anchorage Museum featuring exhibitions including "Michaela Goade: Song of the Seasons," "Everyday Anchorage," and gallery talks. Includes Art Lab and planetarium shorts.

title: Crimson Gallery: 'Bore Tide: Surfing Alaska's Longest Wave'
description: Photography exhibition by Kerry Tasker featuring images of bore tide surfing on Turnagain Arm. Opens during First Friday.

description: The Anchorage Symphony Orchestra performs the original Looney Tunes scores live as classic cartoons play on a big screen, featuring What's Opera Doc?, The Rabbit of Seville, Baton Bunny, and more.

description: The 34th annual Kachemak Bay Shorebird Festival is Alaska's largest wildlife festival, offering nearly 200 events including guided birding trips, expert talks, kayak tours, hands-on workshops, art exhibitions, and activities for all ages during the peak shorebird migration.

title: Kachemak Bay Shorebird Festival
location: Alaska Maritime National Wildlife Refuge Visitor Center, Homer
description: Alaska's largest wildlife festival celebrating the spring shorebird migration, featuring guided birding trips, expert talks, workshops, boat tours, and children's activities for all skill levels. Over 100,000 shorebirds of 25+ species migrate through Kachemak Bay.
